title: AST Trespassing/VCOR: Tok [AK25052058]
summary: On May 30th, 2025, at approximately 1755 hours Tok AST received a report of a trespassing. Investigation found Roylon Mark, age 39 of Tetlin, had committed the crimes of Trespassing II and VCOR x8. He was arrested and remanded to FCC.

He had a $30,000 with 10% posting warrant, a $1000 with 10% posting warrant, and a $100 unsecured warrant. He was tra...

title: New Study Links Saxitoxin to Deaths of Northern Fur Seals in the Southeast Bering Sea
summary: New research published in the journal Marine Mammal Science establishes for the first time a link between the neurotoxin saxitoxin, accumulated during harmful algal blooms, and deaths of laaqudan (in Unangam Tunuu, the first language of Unangax̂ or the Aleut Peoples), or northern fur seals ( Callorhinus ursisnus ), in the southeast Bering Sea. In the study, researchers collected and analyzed data related to the discovery of 10 dead northern fur seals and hundreds of dead, mostly benthic, fish that washed...

title: AST Assault DV: Delta Junction [AK25051899]
summary: On 5-30-25, Delta Junction AST received a report of an assault near Salcha Ave. Investigation showed one party was scratched during an argument which turned slightly physical. A juvenile was present in the residence. Charges are being forwarded over for review and parties separated.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: A new era of political disenfranchisement: soft disenrollment?
summary: Phoenix Johnson Tłingit & Haida In the heart of Southeast Alaska, a quiet storm brews within the Alaskan Native tribal nation of the Central Council of the Tlingit and Haida Indian Tribes, exposing fractures in governance, identity, and power that mirror some of the most dangerous political undercurrents shaping national policy today under Trump. This is not just a story of internal conflict, it’s a warning for Indian Country. The tribe’s executive council quietly rammed through a controversial constitutional rewrite while appearing to sideline valid parliamentary motions that gutted delegations. In the 90th annual tribal assembly that took place April 16-18, an already reduced Seattle delegation of 25 was the sacrificial symbol in a larger campaign...
